Consequently, Paytm Payments Bank Limited is prohibited from conducting the business of  banking  as defined in Section 5(b) or any additional business specified under Section 6 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 with immediate effect. articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India, in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of The Mattancherry Mahajanik Co-operative Urban Bank Ltd, Cochin, Kerala with The Peoples  Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd., Tripunithura, Kerala. articles.title: “Proper Parties” Concept Inapplicable in Election Petitions; CPC is subject to RP Act: J&K and Ladakh HC
articles.summary: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court ruled that the 'Proper Parties' concept under the Code of Civil Procedure (CPC) does not apply in election petitions. The court held that any provision of CPC inconsistent with the Representation of the People (RP) Act cannot override Section 82 of the RP Act, which specifies who must be impleaded as respondents in an election petition. This ruling clarifies the legal framework governing election disputes and ensures adherence to the RP Act's provisions
articles.raw_summary: As per Section 87, RP Act, any provision of CPC that is inconsistent with Section 82, RP Act, which specifies the persons to be impleaded in an election petition, cannot be invoked to implead a person who is otherwise not liable to be joined as a respondent under Section 82, RP Act.  The post  Proper Parties  Concept Inapplicable in Election Petitions; CPC is subject to RP Act: J K and Ladakh HC appeared first on SCC Times .

This policy aims to reduce financial burden on small investors with low balances.

articles.title: RBI allows banks to extend relief measures to borrowers without their requests in disaster-hit areas
articles.summary: New Reserve Bank of India guidelines empower banks to offer relief to all borrowers in calamity-hit regions. These measures, effective July 1, 2026, can be extended without borrower requests. Banks can also operate from temporary premises and set up satellite offices. ATM services will be restored promptly. Relief can include fee waivers for up to a year.

This misuse raises concerns about the law's implementation and its impact on minority rights in tribal areas.

articles.title: No Penalty Where Return Filed After Probate Grant and Income Fully Disclosed in Form 26AS
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that no penalty should be imposed on executors for delayed tax return filing if the delay was due to pending probate proceedings and income was fully disclosed in Form 26AS. This ruling clarifies the tax compliance obligations for individuals handling estate matters where legal proceedings delay tax filings.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that delay in filing the return due to pending probate proceedings was beyond the control of executors. It ruled that such delay constituted a bona fide explanation, leading to deletion of penalty under Section 270A. articles.summary: The Orissa High Court ruled that writ petitions are not maintainable once the Goods and Services Tax Appellate Tribunal (GSTAT) becomes functional, as an effective appellate remedy now exists. The court held that disputes must be pursued through the appellate mechanism rather than continuing with writ petitions. This decision impacts how tax-related disputes are resolved in India, emphasizing the primacy of statutory remedies over constitutional writs.

The ruling underscores the balance between security operations and human rights protections under Indian law.

articles.title: Reopening Invalid Without Independent Material Showing Escapement: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court ruled that tax authorities cannot reopen assessments under Sections 147/148 based solely on third-party data without independent evidence of income escapement. The court stated that such reopenings amount to mere suspicion and lack the required 'reason to believe.' This decision invalidates reassessment proceedings initiated without proper justification, setting a precedent for future tax cases in India.

Key proposals include restricting social media access for children under 16, enhancing AI skills training, and improving governance through technology. The draft policy is open for public feedback before finalization. The initiative seeks to boost innovation while addressing concerns around digital safety and public service delivery.

The draft is open for public feedback.

articles.title: Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that payments for cloud computing services are not taxable as royalty in India, provided no intellectual property rights are transferred. The court clarified that mere access to cloud services does not constitute the use of equipment or technology under tax law. This decision impacts how cloud service providers are taxed and clarifies the legal interpretation of royalty payments in the digital services sector.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court affirmed that payments for cloud computing services are not royalty where no intellectual property rights are transferred. It held that mere access to services does not amount to use of equipment or technology under tax law. The post Cloud Service Payments Not Taxable as Royalty Due to No Transfer of IP Rights: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Roads Cannot Be Blocked In Name Of Religious Activities : SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that roads cannot be blocked in the name of religious activities if secular activities are affected, stating that the government can interfere to protect such rights. The court emphasized that secular activities must not be disrupted under the guise of religious practices. This ruling clarifies the balance between religious freedoms and public order.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court says if a secular activity is being affected, the government can interfere with its rights.

articles.title: Section 80GGC Deduction Denied as Donation Linked to Bogus Political Party Activities
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal upheld the denial of tax deductions under Section 80GGC for donations made to a political party. This ruling underscores that only legitimate donations qualify for tax benefits. The case highlights the need for transparency in political funding to prevent misuse of tax exemptions.

This decision impacts how tax authorities can conduct assessments and reinforces legal safeguards against arbitrary tax demands.

There is also almost no movement through the Strait of Hormuz, the vital bottleneck that's essentially cut off 20% of the world's oil and gas supply. Tuesday, the UAE announced it will leave OPEC, the cartel that has largely controlled oil supplies for decades. Amna Nawaz discussed more with Karen Young.

articles.title: Expected credit loss rules: New provisioning framework to come into force in April 2027
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) will introduce new Expected Credit Loss (ECL) guidelines in April 2027, replacing the current incurred-loss-based provisioning framework with a staging framework for asset classification. The RBI will retain the existing 90-day overdue rule for classifying non-performing assets (NPAs). This change aims to improve risk assessment and provisioning practices in the banking sector.
articles.raw_summary: The new ECL guidelines would introduce a "staging framework" for asset classification under the ECL approach, replacing the existing incurred-loss-based provisioning framework. However, the central bank said in a note that it would retain the current 90-day overdue rule for classifying NPA.

articles.title: Commission Agent Not Eligible for TDS Credit on Farmers’ Income: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that a commission agent cannot claim Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) credit on income earned by farmers. The tribunal stated that TDS credit must align with the person offering the income to tax, emphasizing that the agent does not qualify for such credit. This decision clarifies tax obligations in agricultural transactions and impacts how intermediaries handle farmers' income.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that TDS credit cannot be claimed by an agent when the underlying income belongs to farmers. It ruled that credit must align with the person offering the income to tax. The post Commission Agent Not Eligible for TDS Credit on Farmers’ Income: ITAT Ahmedabad appeared first on TaxGuru .

The sector's struggles highlight potential areas for policy intervention to boost sustainable alternatives.
articles.raw_summary: As industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact, alternative plant fibres are drawing new policy and commercial attention. A FIBRAL report argues diversification will depend on standards, investment and market architecture, not fibre discovery alone at scale.

articles.title: No Reassessment on Mere Change of Opinion: SC Dismisses Revenue’s Plea in Marico Case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled in favor of Marico Ltd, dismissing the Revenue Department's plea to reassess the company's tax filings. The court held that reassessment cannot be initiated merely due to a change of opinion by the Assessing Officer if the issue was already examined during the original assessment. This decision reinforces legal limits on tax reassessment powers.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court in ACIT vs Marico Ltd held that reassessment under Section 148 is invalid where the Assessing Officer had already examined the issue in original assessment proceedings, reaffirming that reopening on a mere change of opinion is without jurisdiction. The post No Reassessment on Mere Change of Opinion: SC Dismisses Revenue’s Plea in Marico Case appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC says law adequate to punish hate speech, underlines enforcement deficit
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India stated that existing laws are sufficient to punish hate speech but highlighted a lack of enforcement. The Court clarified that constitutional courts cannot compel the legislature to create new laws but suggested the government may consider suitable amendments. The ruling underscores the judiciary's role in addressing enforcement gaps in hate speech cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court said constitutional courts cannot compel the legislature to create laws but will be open for the government to consider suitable amendments

articles.title: US sanctions on China's Hengli mark escalation in Iran oil crackdown | Explained
articles.summary: The US imposed sanctions on China's Hengli Petrochemical for allegedly violating Iran oil trade restrictions. Hengli's parent firm denied the allegations, calling the sanctions baseless. The move escalates US pressure on Iran's oil sector and could impact global energy markets, including India's oil supply and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: The firm's Shanghai-listed parent firm Hengli Petrochemical denied doing business with Iran and said the sanctions lack factual and legal basis.

Market borrowing will increase. The current account deficit is projected to widen.

articles.summary: A KPMG report predicts that 48% of government organizations worldwide plan to deploy AI solutions at scale within a year, but 43% face hurdles in scaling these initiatives. The findings, from the 'KPMG Global Tech Report 2026: Government and Public Sector,' suggest growing adoption of AI in governance despite execution challenges. The report underscores the need for robust frameworks to support AI integration in public sector operations.
articles.raw_summary: The report titled "KPMG Global Tech Report 2026: Government and Public Sector" said that 48 per cent of government organisations plan to deploy AI use cases into production at scale within the next 12 months, even as 43 per cent admitted they are facing roadblocks in scaling their initiatives.

High voter participation may reflect public engagement in the electoral process and could influence future governance priorities.

articles.title: Can FTAs drive India’s economic growth
articles.summary: Our experience in the first lot of FTA’s-ASEAN, Japan, South Korea, Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia was not good. articles.title: French regulator says most e-commerce platform products it tested breach EU rules
articles.summary: France's consumer watchdog found that 75% of products tested from online platforms violated EU safety rules, with many posing risks like electric shock or containing harmful chemicals. The regulator shared these findings with the European Commission, suggesting systemic issues in compliance. This highlights broader concerns about product safety and regulation in digital marketplaces, which could influence India's approach to e-commerce and consumer protection policies.
articles.raw_summary: France's consumer watchdog found a staggering 75% of products from online platforms failed EU safety rules. Many items were found to be dangerous, posing risks like electric shock or containing harmful chemicals. This widespread non-compliance suggests systemic issues rather than isolated incidents. The findings will be shared with the European Commission for potential action against platforms.

articles.title: ITR Alone Can’t Prove Low Income to Escape Maintenance; Wife’s Earning Capacity No Bar: Gujarat HC
articles.summary: The Gujarat High Court upheld a ₹50,000 monthly maintenance order, ruling that Income Tax Returns (ITR) alone cannot prove reduced income to avoid payment. The court emphasized that claims of inability must be substantiated with evidence and cannot override maintenance obligations. The judgment also noted that a wife's earning capacity does not bar her from receiving maintenance.
articles.raw_summary: The court upheld ₹50,000 monthly maintenance after finding insufficient evidence of reduced income. It ruled that inability claims must be substantiated and cannot defeat maintenance obligations. articles.title: No Addition if Assessment Based on Mere Guesswork & Suspicion: Chhattisgarh HC
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court ruled that tax authorities cannot make additions to assessments based on mere guesswork or suspicion. The court held that such additions, unsupported by evidence, are invalid and cannot justify rejecting a taxpayer's books. The ruling impacts how tax assessments are conducted, emphasizing the need for concrete evidence over assumptions.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether addition based on assumed production yield is valid. The Court held that without supporting evidence, such additions are unsustainable and cannot justify rejection of books. articles.title: Yotta, Gorilla Technology expand India AI infra pact with $2.8 billion project

This move will significantly boost AI compute capabilities within the country.

The Court proceeded without waiting for the State's response, highlighting concerns over excessive custody periods. This ruling underscores the judiciary's role in safeguarding undertrial rights and may influence future bail policies.

The VC was immediately shut down by the Court staff

articles.title: No Legitimate or Legal Basis for Collection of Badhai Money/Gifts by Transgender Persons: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that there is no legal or legitimate basis for transgender persons to collect 'Badhai money' or gifts, rejecting claims that such collection had become a customary right. The court dismissed the petitioner's argument that the practice had been ongoing for years and should be protected. The ruling highlights the lack of legal recognition for this practice and may impact transgender welfare and rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court rejected the contention that such collection was being made by the petitioner for many years and had translated into a customary right, which should be protected. articles.title: No Capital Gains Tax on Void Sale Deed Executed Through Forged POA: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Ahmedabad ruled that no capital gains tax can be levied if a sale deed is declared void due to a forged Power of Attorney (POA). The case involved a disputed land sale where the tribunal held that once the sale deed is void, no taxable capital gains arise. This decision impacts how tax authorities assess capital gains in cases involving fraudulent property transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved taxation of capital gains based on a disputed land sale. The Tribunal held that once the sale deed was declared void, no capital gains could arise. articles.title: Better preparedness and warning systems needed to deal with ice-rock avalanches

The study emphasizes the need for improved warning systems and preparedness to mitigate such risks in vulnerable regions like the Himalayas.

articles.title: Plastic litter and ghost nets threaten olive ridley hatchlings
articles.summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha is a critical nesting site for olive ridley sea turtles, with nearly 700,000 turtles arriving in February 2025 for mass nesting. Plastic waste and discarded fishing nets (ghost nets) pose severe threats to hatchlings, risking their survival. The article underscores the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect this endangered species and its habitat.
articles.raw_summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha’s Ganjam district is among the most important turtle rookeries in the world. Every year, hundreds of thousands of olive ridley sea turtles (Lepidochelys olivacea) converge here to lay eggs in a synchronous mass nesting event or arribada, with almost 700,000 olive ridleys coming ashore to lay eggs in February 2025 alone. articles.summary: New Delhi has clarified a key point for exporters. Goods moved from SEZ units to the domestic market, after paying duties, will now be considered imported if re-exported. The Iran conflict poses a potential risk.

articles.summary: The US Commerce Department has ordered chip equipment companies to halt shipments to Hua Hong, China's second-largest chipmaker. This follows broader US efforts to restrict China's access to advanced semiconductor technology.

articles.title: Inside WhatsApp's measures to curb digital arrest scams
articles.summary: WhatsApp is using artificial intelligence to tackle digital arrest scams, where cybercriminals impersonate officials to extort victims. The company has banned thousands of fraudulent accounts and is enhancing its AI to detect fake officials and scam patterns, following consultations with Indian government bodies. Telecom measures such as SIM binding are also being implemented to curb fraud. Authorities are working to prevent significant financial losses, though victim recovery remains low.

Authorities are working to prevent significant financial losses to cybercriminals, though victim recovery remains low. Telecom measures like SIM binding are also being implemented to curb fraud.

Officials emphasized local manufacturing and sustainable practices for India's AI future.

articles.title: India industrial output at 4.1%, hit by Iran war
articles.summary: India's industrial output grew 4.1% in March, a five-month low. This slowdown was influenced by weaker electricity and manufacturing sectors. Manufacturing saw a dip, though capital goods showed strong investment demand. Government spending supported infrastructure growth.

Luxury is moving towards access rather than ownership.

articles.title: Explained: What is Mythos AI and why it is worrying banks and regulators
articles.summary: Mythos AI, developed by Anthropic, is an advanced AI tool that can quickly scan systems and identify vulnerabilities much faster than human experts. Its speed and capabilities have raised concerns among banks and regulators, who worry about its potential use as a weapon rather than just a protective tool. The article highlights the dual-use nature of such AI systems and the regulatory challenges they pose.
articles.raw_summary: Mythos AI by Anthropic doesn’t just find flaws, it acts on them. In hours it can scan whole systems and spot cracks that experts might take months to uncover. That speed makes it a powerful shield, but also a tempting weapon—and that’s what has regulators worried.
